title: Trust-Boundary Architecture Plan
consumer_purpose: Eliminate 48 security findings (5 P0, 26 P1, 17 P2) from the trust-boundary audit by hardening capability enforcement, auth data integrity, secret redaction, and validation gates across the plugin and MCP subsystems.
non_duplicated_scope: This plan covers only findings NOT claimed by PLUGIN-TRUTH or MCP-TRUTH lanes: (1) capability boundary `as any` elimination, (2) auth corruption fix, (3) auth response validation, (4) secret redaction in logs, (5) silent catch logging, (6) typeof guard replacement. F01 (process.env to MCP child) is covered by MCP-TRUTH lane and referenced only for env scrubbing coordination.

root_causes.hypothesis: RC1: CapabilityId is a plain string union, not a branded/nominal type — every string literal cast to CapabilityId is accepted by TypeScript
evidence: capability/types.ts:1-17 defines CapabilityId as `(typeof CapabilityId)[keyof typeof CapabilityId]` — a plain string union
hook-dispatch-guard.ts:53,57 uses `requiredCapability as any` and `"network.request" as any`
plugin/index.ts:437 casts HOOK_CAPABILITY_MAP to `Record<string, string>`, discarding CapabilityId
plugin/index.ts:476 passes raw string `capability` to `checkCapability` via `as any`
No Schema.brand anywhere in the capability subsystem — unlike account/schema.ts which uses `Schema.String.pipe(Schema.brand("AccessToken"))`
zoning-board confirms this is a convention violation: branded IDs always use `Schema.String.pipe(Schema.brand(...))`

root_causes.hypothesis: RC2: HTTP response JSON from OAuth providers is cast via `as` without Schema validation — any field can be missing, wrong type, or malicious
evidence: xai.ts:232: `(await response.json()) as DeviceCodeResponse` — no validation
xai.ts:185,202: `response.json() as Promise<TokenResponse>` — no validation
xai.ts:281: `(await response.json()) as TokenResponse` — no validation on success path
xai.ts:283: `(await response.json().catch(() => ({}))) as DeviceTokenErrorBody` — double weakness
codex.ts:583: `(await deviceResponse.json()) as { device_code, ... }` — inline type, no validation
codex.ts:609: `(await response.json()) as { access_token, ... }` — inline type, no validation
copilot.ts:241,270: same pattern
digitalocean.ts:264: `(await res.json().catch(() => undefined)) as { model_routers?: ... }` — cast after undefined fallback
zoning-board confirms secrets should use branded Schema types

root_causes.hypothesis: RC3: Auth data corruption is silently swallowed via `getOrElse(..., () => {})` — all stored tokens are lost without any warning
evidence: mcp/auth.ts:64: `Option.getOrElse(decodeAuthData(data), () => ({}) as AuthData)` — falls back to empty object
mcp/auth.ts:65: `Effect.catch(() => Effect.succeed({} as AuthData))` — second fallback for read errors
No logging at either fallback site
Zoning-board confirms Schema.decodeUnknownOption + Option.getOrElse IS the convention, but silent fallback with no warning is the problem

root_causes.hypothesis: RC4: Silent catch blocks (14+ empty/no-log catches) hide failures that could indicate security issues
evidence: plugin/shared.ts:17-19: `try { return npa(spec) } catch {}` — returns undefined silently
auth/index.ts:59-61: `try { return JSON.parse(...) } catch (err) {}` — silently swallows parse failures
mcp/index.ts:610: `try { process.kill(dpid, 'SIGTERM') } catch {}` — silently swallows kill failures
copilot.ts:146: `} catch {}` during session fetch
copilot.ts:363,388: `.catch(() => undefined)` on HTTP calls
Plus 10 more .catch(() => undefined) or Effect.catch(() => Effect.void) patterns across mcp/index.ts

root_causes.hypothesis: RC5: TypeScript structural typing allows pluginId to be optional — capability checks that depend on identity fail unpredictably
evidence: plugin/index.ts:82: `type ListedPlugin = Hooks & { pluginId?: string }` — optional
plugin/capability/enforcer.ts:26: `registry.get(pluginId)` — if pluginId is missing, returns undefined silently
plugin/capability/hook-dispatch-guard.ts:51: `checkCapability(this.registry, pluginId, ...)` — no undefined guard
Zoning-board confirms PluginSecurityState uses Ref-based registry keyed by string pluginId

root_causes.hypothesis: RC6: Dynamic import of third-party code has no sandbox — but this is a platform-level architectural decision, not a code bug
evidence: loader.ts:141: `mod = await import(row.entry)` — executes arbitrary code in same process
loader.ts:39-41: Loaded type is `Resolved & { mod: Record<string, unknown> }` — no runtime validation of module shape
Node.js vm module, Worker threads, and Deno-style sandboxing are all platform-level changes
Cannot be fixed with Schema validation alone — requires process isolation

fixes.description: Add Schema.brand to CapabilityId, replace all `as any` casts on capability strings with typed lookups, align with codebase branded ID convention

before.plugin/index.ts:476: return yield* checkCapability(s.capabilityRegistry, pluginId, capability as any)

type_errors: Any file using CapabilityId as a plain string union will need to use CapabilityIdValues for literal comparisons
Tests that pass string literals to checkCapability will need updating

risks: CapabilityIdValues literals could diverge from Schema.Literal values — needs a compile-time check
CapabilityId is both exported as const and as type in capability/index.ts:1 — the re-export currently has a bug (`CapabilityId as CapabilityIdValues` on same line), needs fix
External plugin manifests use raw strings — these must go through Schema.decode before becoming CapabilityId
validation: bun typecheck in packages/opencode: should catch any remaining `as any` on capability strings
Unit test: pass invalid capability string → should fail at decode boundary
Unit test: pass valid capability string through Schema.decode → should produce branded CapabilityId
Existing hook dispatch tests should pass unchanged

fixes.description: Replace all `response.json() as { ... }` casts in auth plugins with Schema-validated response types, using branded token schemas

before.codex.ts:583: const deviceData = (await deviceResponse.json()) as { device_code: string; ... }
before.codex.ts:609: const data = (await response.json()) as { access_token: string; ... }
before.copilot.ts:241: const deviceData = (await deviceResponse.json()) as { device_code: string; ... }
before.copilot.ts:270: const data = (await response.json()) as { access_token: string; ... }

after.xai.ts:232 (replaced):
const raw = await response.json()
const json = Schema.decodeUnknownSync(DeviceCodeResponseSchema)(raw)
after.codex.ts (new schemas):
const CodexDeviceCodeSchema = Schema.Struct({ device_code: Schema.String, user_code: Schema.String, verification_uri: Schema.String, expires_in: Schema.Number, interval: Schema.optional(Schema.Number) })
const CodexTokenSchema = Schema.Struct({ access_token: Schema.String.pipe(Schema.brand("CodexAccessToken")), token_type: Schema.String, expires_in: Schema.optional(Schema.Number), refresh_token: Schema.optional(Schema.String), scope: Schema.optional(Schema.String) })
after.copilot.ts (new schemas):
const CopilotDeviceCodeSchema = Schema.Struct({ device_code: Schema.String, user_code: Schema.String, verification_uri: Schema.String, expires_in: Schema.Number, interval: Schema.optional(Schema.Number) })
const CopilotTokenSchema = Schema.Struct({ access_token: Schema.String.pipe(Schema.brand("CopilotAccessToken")), token_type: Schema.String, expires_in: Schema.optional(Schema.Number), refresh_token: Schema.optional(Schema.String), scope: Schema.optional(Schema.String) })

type_errors: Functions consuming the `as`-cast shapes will need to use Schema.Schema.Type<typeof Schema>
TokenResponse type usages across files must be updated to use schema-derived types

risks: CRITICAL EDGE CASE (building-inspector E6-01): OAuth providers add new fields over time (e.g., OpenAI added `id_token` later). Schemas MUST use `Schema.ignoreExtra()` or `Schema.Struct(...).pipe(Schema.ignoreExtra())` for forward compatibility, otherwise all future provider updates break auth.

CRITICAL EDGE CASE (building-inspector E6-02): Rate-limit error responses have different shapes than success responses (e.g., `{ error: 'rate_limited', retry_after: 30 }` vs `{ access_token: '...' }`). Schema must use discriminated union or optional fields to handle both success and error shapes.

Schema.decodeUnknownSync throws on failure — callers currently don't handle this; need to decide: throw (fail fast) vs decodeUnknownOption (graceful). Recommendation: use decodeUnknownSync for success-path validation; add separate error-shape decoding for error paths.
DeviceTokenErrorBody in xai.ts:283 is used with `.catch(() => ({}))` — the fallback to empty object breaks if we validate strictly; needs decodeUnknownOption instead
validation: Test: valid OAuth token response → Schema.decodeUnknownSync succeeds
Test: missing access_token in response → decode fails with ParseError
Test: extra unexpected field in response → succeeds with ignoreExtra
Test: malformed JSON response → decode fails

fixes.description: Add warning logging on auth data corruption instead of silent fallback to empty object; preserve partial data when possible

type_errors: None expected — the Interface type stays the same (still returns Record<string, Entry>)

risks: Partial recovery iterates Object.entries on potentially large objects — acceptably small for auth data (few entries)
Effect.logWarning is used inside Effect.gen — needs `yield*` (or use `Effect.fn` wrapper which it already has)
The `_tag` field on AuthStoreCorrupted is unused here — could be removed if no consumer needs it

CRITICAL EDGE CASE (building-inspector E4-01): Missing auth file (ENOENT on first launch) must NOT log a warning — it is normal. Must distinguish ENOENT (first launch) from corrupt JSON (real corruption). Suggestion: check if file exists before read; only warn on parse/decode failures, not on missing file.

CRITICAL EDGE CASE (building-inspector E4-02): Current decodeAuthData decodes entire file atomically — if one entry is corrupt, ALL entries are discarded. The partial recovery fix addresses this by iterating per-key.
validation: Test: corrupted JSON auth file → logs warning, returns empty object
Test: partially corrupted auth file (some entries valid) → logs warning, returns recovered entries
Test: completely valid auth file → no warning, returns all entries
Test: missing auth file → no warning, returns empty object
Test: disk read error → logs warning, returns empty object

fixes.description: Add centralized secret redaction for log statements, error messages, and debug output; apply to oauth-provider.ts token logging; coordinate env scrubbing with MCP-TRUTH lane via existing sanitizeEnv
fixes.coordinates_with: MCP-TRUTH lane for F01 (env scrubbing before MCP child spawn)

after.oauth-provider.ts:127 (replaced): log.info("saved oauth tokens", { mcpName: this.mcpName, hasAccessToken: !!entry?.tokens?.accessToken, hasRefreshToken: !!entry?.tokens?.refreshToken })
after.oauth-provider.ts:142 (replaced): log.info("refreshed oauth tokens", { mcpName: this.mcpName, hasAccessToken: !!tokens?.access_token, hasRefreshToken: !!tokens?.refresh_token })

type_errors: None expected — stripSecrets interface stays the same

risks: The stripSecrets string-matching heuristic is fragile — `title` matches `title` (no!) — current SECRET_PATTERNS does NOT include `title`, but `key` matches any key containing 'key' (e.g., `keyboard`, `monkey`). This is existing behavior, not worsened by this fix.

Zoning-board flagged this as a convention violation: no other part of the codebase uses string-matching for secret detection. Full fix (Schema-branded tokens) deferred to a separate secrets-policy milestone (RL-004).
redactSecrets recursion depth is bounded by object nesting depth in log contexts (typically 1-2 levels)
validation: Test: stripSecrets removes apiKey, token, password fields from config
Test: oauth-provider log statements do NOT contain access_token values
Test: oauth-provider log statements DO contain boolean hasAccessToken flags
Verify no access_token or refresh_token values appear in any log output

fixes.description: Add warning/error logging to all 14+ silent catch blocks; use Effect.logWarning for non-critical paths, Effect.logError for auth/data paths

tests_to_run: All existing MCP and plugin tests

risks: Effect.logDebug inside Effect.gen requires yield* — the process.kill catch block at mcp/index.ts:610 is inside a for loop within Effect.gen, which already has yield* context

CRITICAL EDGE CASE (building-inspector E5-02): The npa parse catch at shared.ts:17 is intentionally silent (non-npm specifiers like file paths are expected to fail npa parsing, and parsePluginSpecifier handles undefined). Adding logging here would produce false-positive noise. FIX: add comment only, not logging.

CRITICAL EDGE CASE (building-inspector E5-01): Adding logging to WebSocket/event-loop hot paths causes latency/backpressure — identify and skip logging for high-frequency catch sites like hook dispatch loops. Use log.debug (not warn) for expected failures like process already dead.
Some .catch(() => undefined) patterns on HTTP calls (copilot.ts:363,388) are expected graceful fallbacks — these should use log.debug, not log.warn (normal when tokens are not yet available)
validation: Test: corrupt OPENCODE_AUTH_CONTENT → warning logged, falls back to file
Test: MCP client close with already-dead process → debug log, no crash
Test: valid redirect URI → no warning logged
Test: invalid redirect URI → warning logged with the invalid URI (NOT the error, which may contain sensitive data)
Verify no silent empty catch {} blocks remain in audited files

fixes.description: Replace `typeof x === 'object'` guards on OAuth config with Schema validation using the existing ConfigMCP.OAuth schema

before.mcp/index.ts:332: const oauthConfig = typeof mcp.oauth === "object" ? mcp.oauth : undefined
before.mcp/index.ts:851: const oauthConfig = typeof mcpConfig.oauth === "object" ? mcpConfig.oauth : undefined

type_errors: ConfigMCP.OAuth is already imported via ConfigMCP at line 16 — no new imports needed
mcp.oauth is typed as `ConfigMCP.OAuth | false | undefined` at the config level, but the `typeof` guard widens this — Schema.decodeUnknownOption will narrow correctly

risks: ConfigMCP.OAuth has optional fields (clientId, clientSecret, scope, callbackPort, redirectUri) — an empty object `{}` passes validation, which is the correct behavior (same as typeof guard)
If `mcp.oauth` is `false` (explicitly disabled), Schema.decodeUnknownOption returns None → falls back to undefined, which is correct (preserves existing behavior)

Null values: `typeof null === 'object'` is true in JS but `null` will fail Schema.decodeUnknownOption → returns undefined. This is an IMPROVEMENT over the current behavior which would pass null through as an object.
validation: Test: valid OAuth config object → Schema decode succeeds, config returned
Test: oauth: false → Schema decode returns None → undefined
Test: oauth: undefined → Schema decode returns None → undefined
Test: oauth: null → Schema decode returns None → undefined (BETTER than current typeof guard which would accept null)
Test: oauth: 'string' → Schema decode returns None → undefined
Test: oauth: {} → Schema decode succeeds, empty config returned

fixes.description: Make pluginId required on ListedPlugin; add identity loss warnings when pluginId is missing in dispatch pipeline

before.enforcer.ts:26: if (!state) return Effect.succeed(false)  // unknown plugin — silently denied
before.hook-dispatch-guard.ts:46-47: if (!requiredCapability) return Effect.succeed(false)  // unknown hook — silently denied, no logging

type_errors: Any code constructing ListedPlugin without pluginId will fail typecheck
getLegacyPlugins() and applyPlugin() must ensure pluginId is always set

risks: Legacy plugins discovered via dynamic import may not export an `id` field — need fallback generation
Built-in plugins (internalPlugins()) already have explicit ids set in their PluginInstance shapes — no risk
Plugin ID is used as a Map key in the Registry — changing from optional to required means we must ensure it's always a non-empty string
The `shared.ts:253-258 readPluginId()` function validates and normalizes IDs — this should be the single source of truth
validation: Test: legacy plugin without id → fallback id generated, warning logged
Test: built-in plugin → uses its declared id
Test: npm plugin with id → id preserved
Test: duplicate ids → existing behavior preserved (last write wins in Map)
bun typecheck → no `pluginId?: string` remain in plugin dispatch pipeline

apply_order_rationale: A first (types must be right before casts can be removed). G second (pluginId must be required before capability checks depend on it). F third (typeof guards replaced with Schema — validates the Schema pipeline before auth schemas are added). C fourth (auth corruption logging before auth response schemas to establish error handling pattern). B fifth (auth response schemas added after error handling is in place). D sixth (secret redaction after all logging is in place). E last (silent catch logging is the most mechanical fix, applied last to avoid merge conflicts with other edits).
rollback_plan: Revert each fix in reverse order (E → D → B → C → F → G → A). Each fix is independently revertible — A and G change types but the other fixes are additive logging/validation only.

deferred_risks.rationale: Platform-level change requiring Worker threads, node:vm, or process isolation. Not a code-level fix. Deferred to sandboxing milestone.
deferred_risks.mitigation: Document in trust-boundary security model doc. Plugin review process should include code audit before approval.

deferred_risks.rationale: Public OAuth clients for desktop flows are intentional — the client ID is public by design in the OAuth PKCE flow. Moving to config would break the headless device-code flow which depends on known client IDs.
deferred_risks.mitigation: Document rationale in security model. Add comment at each CLIENT_ID constant explaining it is a public OAuth client identifier.

deferred_risks.rationale: Encrypting the auth file requires key management (where do you store the encryption key?). The file is already written with 0o600 permissions. Full disk encryption is the user's responsibility.
deferred_risks.mitigation: Add comment in auth.ts explaining the security model: file permissions (0o600) protect against other users on the same machine; full disk encryption is expected for physical security.

deferred_risks.rationale: Current heuristic uses key name substring matching (e.g., 'key' matches any key containing 'key'). Zoning-board flagged as convention violation. Full fix requires Schema-branded token types throughout. Deferred to RL-004 secrets-policy milestone.
deferred_risks.mitigation: Enhance SECRET_PATTERNS to include env-blocklist suffixes (_TOKEN, _KEY, _SECRET, _PASSWORD, _CREDENTIAL, _API_KEY). Add word-boundary matching to prevent false positives like 'keyboard'.

deferred_risks.risk: OPENCODE_AUTH_CONTENT leaks full auth payload to MCP child processes (control-plane/workspace.ts:571)
deferred_risks.rationale: Discovered by load-bearer downstream trace. workspace.ts:571 serializes ALL auth tokens into OPENCODE_AUTH_CONTENT env var, which is inherited by every MCP child process via ...process.env at mcp/index.ts:456. This is the single largest secret leak.
deferred_risks.mitigation: Add OPENCODE_AUTH_CONTENT to env-blocklist. MCP-TRUTH lane env scrubbing will strip it.

deferred_risks.rationale: SENSITIVE_PATTERNS redaction list does not include refresh, access, or standalone key — Auth.Info values containing access_tokens may pass unredacted in debug exports.
deferred_risks.mitigation: Add refresh, access, key to SENSITIVE_PATTERNS in debug-packet.ts.

deferred_risks.risk: MCP-TRUTH lane coordination — env scrubbing before child spawn (F01)
deferred_risks.rationale: F01 (...process.env passed to every MCP child) is owned by MCP-TRUTH lane. This plan coordinates by ensuring the sanitizeEnv import path and redaction function are available for MCP-TRUTH to use.
deferred_risks.mitigation: Export redactSecrets from plugin/index.ts or a shared utility. MCP-TRUTH will use sanitizeEnv from @opencode-ai/core/util/env-blocklist for the actual env scrubbing at mcp/index.ts:456.

convention_compliance.tagged_errors: AuthStoreCorrupted in fix-C follows the _tag convention but is deliberately NOT a Schema.TaggedErrorClass (it's not thrown — it's used for logging context). This is intentional.
convention_compliance.effect_logging: fix-C and fix-G use Effect.logWarning/Effect.logInfo + Effect.annotateLogs — matches session/llm.ts pattern
convention_compliance.schema_decode: fix-F uses Schema.decodeUnknownOption + Option.getOrElse — matches mcp/auth.ts pattern
convention_compliance.service_methods: fix-G wraps checkCapability in Effect.gen (already done in plugin/index.ts:474). The standalone checkCapability in enforcer.ts remains a bare function (not a service method) — this is acceptable as a utility, not a service method, but zoning-board flagged it. Deferred to refactoring milestone.

out_of_scope_findings_recorded: capability/index.ts:1 re-export has duplicate key (CapabilityId as CapabilityIdValues on same line) — needs correction
enforcer.ts uses Effect.flatMap instead of Effect.gen — convention violation per zoning-board
stripSecrets string-matching heuristic is fragile (matches 'key' in 'keyboard') — deferred to RL-004
